methods.method_name: Batch Processing Strategy for HMM Layer Detection
methods.description: Strategy to divide large ice core data series into smaller, overlapping batches, apply the Hidden Markov Model (HMM) layer detection algorithm to each batch individually, and then stitch the results together by convolving the resulting layer probability distributions. This approach is used to handle non-stationarity in the data and manage computational complexity.

steps.description: divide the total data series into smaller batches
steps.searchable_summary: divide time series data into smaller segments or batches

steps.description: apply the layer detection algorithm to one of these at a time
steps.searchable_summary: apply Hidden Markov Model algorithm for layer detection to each data batch

steps.description: subsequently stitch them together by convolving the resulting layer probability distributions
steps.searchable_summary: combine layer probability distributions from successive batches using convolution

steps.description: choosing data batches in consecutive order and in an overlapping fashion
steps.searchable_summary: select data batches with overlap for sequential processing

steps.description: some of the information inferred from the layering in one batch of data may be incorporated into the next
steps.searchable_summary: incorporate results or information from previous batch into processing of the next batch

methods.description: Process to optimize the parameters (θ) of the Hidden Markov Model used for annual layer detection. This is achieved by evaluating the likelihood of the current parameters based on the appearance of inferred layers in the observation sequence and iteratively updating the parameters using the Expectation-Maximization (EM) algorithm. This allows the model to adapt to temporal changes in annual layer appearance and thickness distribution.

steps.description: evaluating the likelihood of the employed set of model parameters based on the appearance of inferred layers in the observation sequence
steps.searchable_summary: evaluate likelihood of HMM parameters based on inferred layer probabilities from Forward-Backward algorithm output

steps.description: By normalizing the posterior probabilities, the likelihood of the applied model parameters can be determined
steps.searchable_summary: normalize posterior probabilities to determine parameter likelihood

steps.description: implement a learning process, which can select the model parameters best suited for modelling the observations
steps.searchable_summary: implement iterative parameter optimization using Expectation-Maximization (EM) algorithm

steps.description: The EM algorithm presents an efficient iterative method for obtaining a point estimate of the (possibly local) maximum likelihood set of parameters. See Appendix A for update equations of the respective model parameters.
steps.searchable_summary: iteratively update HMM model parameters using EM update equations
